name: ION-16032 Template Jetty 400 QA Investigation
description: Investigating why QA still gets HTTP 400 for template names with / after the UriCompliance fix was deployed

entries.summary: ROOT CAUSE: Jetty 12 ee10 Servlet layer (ServletContextRequest) performs a SECOND, independent URI violation check that ignores connector-level UriCompliance configuration
entries.detail:
The deployed fix sets UriCompliance.DEFAULT.with("BOOKING", AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR) on HttpConnectorFactory — this works at the Jetty HTTP layer. HOWEVER, the ee10 Servlet layer (ServletContextRequest) does a separate check: httpURI.hasViolations() → then checks if UriCompliance.AMBIGUOUS_VIOLATIONS.contains(violation) → if YES and ServletHandler.isDecodeAmbiguousURIs() is FALSE (default), it throws AmbiguousURI error (400).

Evidence from QA logs:
- OPTIONS /booking/template/BK_Test%2F001 → 400 (with %2F, 0 bytes body, 3ms)
- OPTIONS /booking/template/Arijit-test-001 → 200 (without %2F, works fine)
- Requests with %2F never reach Jersey (no MercuryRequestLoggingFilter log)
- UriCompliance IS configured (confirmed in startup log at 13:26 UTC)
- Routes DO show {name: .+} (confirmed in route listing)

FIX NEEDED: In addition to the current UriCompliance config, must also call:
environment.getApplicationContext().getServletHandler().setDecodeAmbiguousURIs(true)

This is the ee10 Servlet 6.0 compliance enforcement — it's independent of the Jetty HTTP connector compliance. See Jetty GitHub issue #11298.

entries.summary: User confirmed findings, requested documentation before code change
entries.detail: User reviewed findings. Next steps: 1) Document full investigation in booking/docs/2026-06-17-booking-template-jetty-issue-part2.md. 2) Apply fix locally on previous branch. 3) Test/debug locally before pushing to QA. Documenting first for user review.

entries.summary: Documentation complete, awaiting user review before applying fix locally
entries.detail: Created comprehensive investigation document at booking/docs/2026-06-17-booking-template-jetty-issue-part2.md. Document covers: all AWS CLI queries used, Jetty bytecode analysis steps, root cause explanation (dual-layer compliance in Jetty 12 ee10), architecture diagram, required fix (setDecodeAmbiguousURIs), security assessment, testing plan, and timeline. Awaiting user review before applying code changes.
